channel.title: Light Tower Group — NYC CRE Capital Markets Insights
channel.description: Data-driven capital markets analysis and building intelligence for NYC commercial real estate professionals.

item.title: A 10-Year Lease at $85 PSF Just Tested What Office Tenants Will Pay for Stability
item.description: Fidelity National Title Insurance signs 20K SF lease at SL Green's 711 Third Avenue. The 10-year term at Midtown East's average $85 PSF reveals the underwriting margin tenants now demand.

news:news.news:title: A 10-Year Lease at $85 PSF Just Tested What Office Tenants Will Pay for Stability
news:news.news:keywords: SL Green, Fidelity National Title, 711 Third Avenue, office leasing, Midtown East

item.title: New Mexico SIC Is Hiring. The Real Signal Is What It Already Owns.
item.description: New Mexico SIC seeks a real estate and real asset analyst. The hire signals portfolio complexity and manager oversight needs, not a new allocation mandate.

news:news.news:title: New Mexico SIC Is Hiring. The Real Signal Is What It Already Owns.
news:news.news:keywords: New Mexico SIC, sovereign wealth fund, real estate analyst, institutional capital, portfolio management, manager oversight

item.title: KREF Is Not Just Selling. It Is Admitting the Model Broke.
item.description: KKR Real Estate Finance Trust posts $122M loss, explores sale. The strategic review signals more than distress: it reveals the structural limits of floating-rate CRE lending in a reset market.

news:news.news:title: KREF Is Not Just Selling. It Is Admitting the Model Broke.
news:news.news:keywords: KREF, KKR Real Estate Finance Trust, CRE lending, strategic alternatives, floating-rate debt, loan portfolio, distress, liquidity

item.title: JPMorgan's $425M Third Avenue Exit: The Basis Is the Deal, Not the Building
item.description: JPMorgan Asset Management seeks to sell its 49% stake in Fisher Brothers' 605 Third Avenue at a $425M valuation, down from a $600M ask in 2020. The deal tests whether office capital is returning or just concentrating.

news:news.news:title: JPMorgan's $425M Third Avenue Exit: The Basis Is the Deal, Not the Building
news:news.news:keywords: JPMorgan, Fisher Brothers, 605 Third Avenue, office recapitalization, New York City office market

item.title: Can a $2.7M Oxnard Mansion Prove Waterfront Value Exists Outside the Premium Bidding War?
item.description: A $2.695M Oxnard mansion with a private boat dock lists at a fraction of comparable LA or Miami waterfront. The basis gap signals where capital is rotating for yield.

news:news.news:title: Can a $2.7M Oxnard Mansion Prove Waterfront Value Exists Outside the Premium Bidding War?
news:news.news:keywords: waterfront real estate, Oxnard, basis gap, secondary market, lifestyle asset, capital rotation

item.title: Mamdani's EDC Picks: The Clock on City Land Deals Just Got More Expensive
item.description: Mayor Mamdani names Anthony Shorris CEO and Lina Khan chair of NYCEDC after a 7-month delay. The appointment signals a shift in how city land deals are structured, compressing the timeline for developers.

news:news.news:title: Mamdani's EDC Picks: The Clock on City Land Deals Just Got More Expensive
news:news.news:keywords: NYCEDC, Anthony Shorris, Lina Khan, Zohran Mamdani, city land deals

item.title: 25.3 Acres of Concrete Paved the Way for This Laredo IOS Deal
item.description: Realterm buys 40-acre IOS facility in Laredo, TX. The 25.3 concrete-paved acres and Heartland Express lease reveal how infrastructure scarcity drives industrial outdoor storage pricing.

news:news.news:title: 25.3 Acres of Concrete Paved the Way for This Laredo IOS Deal
news:news.news:keywords: industrial outdoor storage, Realterm, Laredo, Heartland Express, transit-oriented properties

item.title: The Plaza at Normandy Sale: A 1031 Buyer Paid Full Basis for a 2024 Build
item.description: TSCG arranges $20.7M sale of Publix-anchored shopping center in Jacksonville. A 1031 buyer pays full basis for a 2024-vintage, fully leased asset. What this signals about retail capital demand.

news:news.news:title: The Plaza at Normandy Sale: A 1031 Buyer Paid Full Basis for a 2024 Build
news:news.news:keywords: Publix, Jacksonville, 1031 exchange, grocery-anchored retail, TSCG

item.title: Can a $300 Million Infrastructure Check Buy What a Pension Fund Really Needs?
item.description: Maine PERS commits $300M to Brookfield Super-Core Infrastructure Partners. The allocation reveals how pension funds are trading liquidity for yield as time becomes the most expensive ingredient in infrastructure capital.

news:news.news:title: Can a $300 Million Infrastructure Check Buy What a Pension Fund Really Needs?
news:news.news:keywords: Maine PERS, Brookfield, infrastructure, pension funds, capital allocation

item.title: Maroa Park Sale: The Fresno Multifamily Bid Is Real, but Narrow
item.description: JBT Property Management sells 248-unit Maroa Park Apartments in Fresno for $44 million. The trade reveals a narrow bid for multifamily in secondary markets.

news:news.news:title: Maroa Park Sale: The Fresno Multifamily Bid Is Real, but Narrow
news:news.news:keywords: Fresno, multifamily, JBT Property Management, Mogharebi Group, private buyer

item.title: State of Utah Buys Franklin Campus: The Buyer Is the Only Buyer That Matters
item.description: Utah pays $40M for Franklin Campus at $128/sf. The state's cost of capital and mission-driven demand reveal a new pricing tier in office.

news:news.news:title: State of Utah Buys Franklin Campus: The Buyer Is the Only Buyer That Matters
news:news.news:keywords: office, government buyer, Utah, basis reset, public sector, Salt Lake City, FranklinCovey

item.title: Can a Golf Resort Sale Prove Time Is the Most Expensive Ingredient in CRE?
item.description: Ohana Real Estate Investors pays $191M for Panther National golf resort. The deal reveals how time, leverage, and execution risk shaped Centaur's exit and J.P. Morgan's underwriting.

news:news.news:title: Can a Golf Resort Sale Prove Time Is the Most Expensive Ingredient in CRE?
news:news.news:keywords: golf resort, Palm Beach Gardens, Centaur Holdings, Ohana Real Estate Investors, J.P. Morgan Chase, acquisition financing, master-planned development, time as cost of capital

item.title: Bascom Buys Castlewood Park: The Basis Is the Deal
item.description: Bascom Group pays $53.1M for a 183-unit Orange County complex. The $290,200 per-unit price and 30-year seller hold reveal a market where basis discipline, not yield chasing, drives multifamily acquisitions.

news:news.news:title: Bascom Buys Castlewood Park: The Basis Is the Deal
news:news.news:keywords: Bascom Group, Orange County, multifamily, basis, renovation, supply constraint, capital markets

item.title: JP Morgan Financed a 40-Property Industrial Portfolio. Here's What They Had to Believe.
item.description: JP Morgan provides $208.5M floating-rate loan to recapitalize 40 industrial assets. The 95% occupancy and 110-tenant roster reveal the underwriting conditions that separate investable deals from attractive stories.

news:news.news:title: JP Morgan Financed a 40-Property Industrial Portfolio. Here's What They Had to Believe.
news:news.news:keywords: JP Morgan, industrial, Centerbridge Partners, Henderson Group, floating-rate loan, recapitalization, logistics

item.title: Lenders Are Underwriting Stabilized Multifamily on Lease-Up Risk, Not Location
item.description: PCCP provides $51.8M senior loan for Everly at Morrison Ranch in Gilbert, AZ. The 97%-occupied 2025-vintage asset shows lenders now underwrite lease-up execution, not just market location.

news:news.news:title: Lenders Are Underwriting Stabilized Multifamily on Lease-Up Risk, Not Location
news:news.news:keywords: PCCP, Phoenix Capital Management, P.B. Bell, Everly at Morrison Ranch, multifamily refinancing, Gilbert Arizona, lease-up risk, stabilized multifamily, senior loan, Class A apartments

item.title: What Does a 217-Acre Office Campus Sale Say About Corporate HQ Liquidity?
item.description: King Realty Advisors and Colliers arrange sale of 217-acre Emerson Electric campus in St. Louis. The deal reveals how corporate HQ liquidity depends on tenant credit, basis reset, and sponsor patience.

news:news.news:title: What Does a 217-Acre Office Campus Sale Say About Corporate HQ Liquidity?
news:news.news:keywords: office, St. Louis, corporate headquarters, liquidity, basis, sale-leaseback, Emerson Electric, Copeland, Blackstone, Jim Onder

item.title: Wawa Paid $1,576 a Foot. The Market Is Paying for Time.
item.description: Marcus & Millichap brokers $8.8M sale of a 5,585 SF Wawa in Edison, NJ. The price per square foot signals how net-lease capital is underwriting replacement cost and lease term, not just location.

news:news.news:title: Wawa Paid $1,576 a Foot. The Market Is Paying for Time.
news:news.news:keywords: net-lease, Wawa, Edison, Marcus & Millichap, retail-capital-markets

item.title: What an $8.5M Grocery Sale in Beverly Tells Owners About Net-Lease Pricing
item.description: JLL sells a 60,666-sf Stop & Shop in Beverly, MA for $8.5M. The price per square foot and lease structure reveal how net-lease capital is pricing duration over location.

news:news.news:title: What an $8.5M Grocery Sale in Beverly Tells Owners About Net-Lease Pricing
news:news.news:keywords: net lease, grocery-anchored retail, Stop & Shop, Beverly MA, JLL, single-tenant, basis reset, lease duration

item.title: JLL's Dallas Deal Shows Construction Debt Is Available Only for the Right Sponsor Stack
item.description: JLL arranges construction debt and JV equity for 8300 Douglas in Dallas. The deal reveals that construction lending is open only to projects with a credit tenant, proven developers, and a multi-use structure that spreads risk.

news:news.news:title: JLL's Dallas Deal Shows Construction Debt Is Available Only for the Right Sponsor Stack
news:news.news:keywords: construction debt, joint venture equity, Dallas, mixed-use, RAMROCK Real Estate, Lincoln Property Co., Fifth Third Bank, JLL

item.title: Lina Khan at EDC: Time Is Now the Most Expensive Ingredient in NYC Development
item.description: Lina Khan's appointment as EDC board chair signals that entitlement risk and regulatory timeline are becoming the dominant variables in New York development capital.

news:news.news:title: Lina Khan at EDC: Time Is Now the Most Expensive Ingredient in NYC Development
news:news.news:keywords: Lina Khan, NYC EDC, development policy, entitlement risk, New York City real estate

item.title: A $621 Million Loan Closed. Here's What M&T Had to Believe.
item.description: JLL arranges $621M refinancing for Merritt Properties' 58-building Maryland industrial portfolio via M&T Bank. The seven-year balance sheet loan shows what regional banks require to lend at scale.

news:news.news:title: A $621 Million Loan Closed. Here's What M&T Had to Believe.
news:news.news:keywords: industrial, refinancing, M&T Bank, Merritt Properties, JLL, Baltimore-Washington Corridor, balance sheet lending, regional banks

item.title: $133M Providence Place Mall Deal: The Price Is the Story, Not the Mall
item.description: Pyramid, Paolino, and DW Partners acquire Providence Place Mall for $133M. The price reveals how capital is pricing regional mall risk after years of receivership.

news:news.news:title: $133M Providence Place Mall Deal: The Price Is the Story, Not the Mall
news:news.news:keywords: regional mall, receivership, Brookfield Properties, Pyramid Management Group, retail distress, capital markets

item.title: What HDR's 74,500-SF Lease at 7 Penn Plaza Tells Office Owners About Tenant Demand
item.description: HDR signs 74,500 SF lease at 7 Penn Plaza. The deal shows office demand is concentrated among firms that consume their own space, not speculative tenants.

news:news.news:title: What HDR's 74,500-SF Lease at 7 Penn Plaza Tells Office Owners About Tenant Demand
news:news.news:keywords: office leasing, Midtown Manhattan, HDR, Feil Organization, tenant demand, 7 Penn Plaza, New York City office market

item.title: A Lender Just Funded Condo Construction in Harlem. Here's What They Had to Believe.
item.description: Mass Development's $45M construction loan for 72 condos in Harlem reveals lender conviction on for-sale product, basis, and timeline in a cautious market.

news:news.news:title: A Lender Just Funded Condo Construction in Harlem. Here's What They Had to Believe.
news:news.news:keywords: construction financing, Harlem, condominium, Mass Development, New York City development, for-sale residential, lender conviction

item.title: A 50% Leased Office Building Just Got $228.9 Million in Fresh Debt
item.description: PGIM, Tribeca, and Meadow Partners secured $228.9M from Rialto and Hines to refinance 295 Fifth Ave, a 50% leased office building. The deal signals lender appetite for repositioned assets with strong sponsorship.

news:news.news:title: A 50% Leased Office Building Just Got $228.9 Million in Fresh Debt
news:news.news:keywords: office refinancing, bridge debt, Rialto Capital, Hines, 295 Fifth Avenue, Midtown South, PGIM

item.title: Developers Are Building Multifamily in San Antonio Without Disclosing Rents
item.description: Rosewood Property Co. begins leasing 359 units in San Antonio's Alamo Heights. The absence of disclosed rents signals a market where construction lenders are underwriting on location and sponsor quality, not on proven rent levels.

news:news.news:title: Developers Are Building Multifamily in San Antonio Without Disclosing Rents
news:news.news:keywords: Rosewood Property Co., San Antonio, multifamily development, construction lending, Tobin Estates

item.title: The Deal Looked Like a Sale. It Was a Risk-Allocation Decision.
item.description: Marcus & Millichap sells 72%-occupied Commerce Centre in Prairieville, LA. The deal reveals how lenders are allocating risk in secondary retail.

news:news.news:title: The Deal Looked Like a Sale. It Was a Risk-Allocation Decision.
news:news.news:keywords: retail, capital markets, lending, risk allocation, Marcus & Millichap

item.title: Can a 450-Unit Rental Tower in Downtown Brooklyn Really Lease 430 Market-Rate Units at Today's Rents?
item.description: The Fleet's 450-unit tower nears completion in Downtown Brooklyn. With only 20 affordable units, the market must absorb 430 market-rate rentals. Can rents hold?

news:news.news:title: Can a 450-Unit Rental Tower in Downtown Brooklyn Really Lease 430 Market-Rate Units at Today's Rents?
news:news.news:keywords: Brooklyn, multifamily, rental, supply, Downtown Brooklyn, Jay Group

item.title: SEBCO's Bronx Senior Housing Closes the Gap Between Subsidy and Construction Cost
item.description: SEBCO completes 134-unit senior housing in the Bronx at 130% AMI. The project reveals how low land basis, subsidy, and patient capital close the gap between construction cost and regulated rent.

news:news.news:title: SEBCO's Bronx Senior Housing Closes the Gap Between Subsidy and Construction Cost
news:news.news:keywords: affordable housing, Bronx, development, senior housing, capital stack

item.title: Private Credit Lends on Self-Storage, but the Borrower Takes the Rate Risk
item.description: ACORE Capital provides $140M floating-rate loan to refinance a 7,650-unit self-storage portfolio. The deal shows private credit's appetite for new supply and the constraints of floating-rate debt.

news:news.news:title: Private Credit Lends on Self-Storage, but the Borrower Takes the Rate Risk
news:news.news:keywords: self-storage, ACORE Capital, Ardent Companies, StepStone Real Estate, floating-rate debt, private credit, refinancing, JLL Capital Markets

item.title: Intercontinental's $69.5M Buy Tests How Long a Retail Income Stream Must Be to Clear
item.description: Intercontinental Real Estate pays $69.5M for fully leased Lakeland Town Center near Seattle. The deal tests whether grocery-anchored retail pricing now depends on lease duration and sponsor patience, not just location.

news:news.news:title: Intercontinental's $69.5M Buy Tests How Long a Retail Income Stream Must Be to Clear
news:news.news:keywords: grocery-anchored retail, Intercontinental Real Estate, Seattle retail, Lakeland Town Center, retail investment

item.title: $200,000 Per Door in Wausau: What a 2020-Vintage Multifamily Sale Reveals About Secondary Market Pricing
item.description: Marcus & Millichap brokers $15M sale of 75-unit multifamily in Wausau, WI. At $200K/door, the deal reveals how vintage, basis, and local demand shape pricing in secondary markets.

news:news.news:title: $200,000 Per Door in Wausau: What a 2020-Vintage Multifamily Sale Reveals About Secondary Market Pricing
news:news.news:keywords: multifamily, secondary markets, Wausau, Marcus & Millichap, pricing

item.title: A Vacant Building Just Sold for $15.2M in Lower Manhattan. Here's What the Buyer Had to Believe.
item.description: Matthews negotiates $15.2M sale of vacant 19,873 SF mixed-use building at 158 Lafayette St., Lower Manhattan. Analysis of basis, redevelopment optionality, and capital market signal.

news:news.news:title: A Vacant Building Just Sold for $15.2M in Lower Manhattan. Here's What the Buyer Had to Believe.
news:news.news:keywords: Lower Manhattan, mixed-use, redevelopment, basis, vacant building

item.title: Can Affordable Housing Development Survive When the Subsidy Clock Runs Faster Than the Deal?
item.description: Beacon Communities plans a $63M, 72-unit affordable project in Newton. The deal hinges on LIHTC timing, city funds, and a 2027 purchase deadline.

news:news.news:title: Can Affordable Housing Development Survive When the Subsidy Clock Runs Faster Than the Deal?
news:news.news:keywords: affordable housing, LIHTC, Beacon Communities, Newton, MBTA Communities Act, development finance

item.title: Lenders Are Underwriting Brooklyn Multifamily on Tax Exemption Duration, Not Rent Growth
item.description: GO Residential REIT's $109M acquisition of 186 units at 409 Eastern Parkway shows lenders are pricing tax-exemption duration into multifamily debt underwriting.

news:news.news:title: Lenders Are Underwriting Brooklyn Multifamily on Tax Exemption Duration, Not Rent Growth
news:news.news:keywords: GO Residential REIT, Brooklyn multifamily, 421-a tax exemption, Crown Heights, multifamily debt underwriting

item.title: Multifamily Lending Grew 53%. The Real Signal Is in What Shrank.
item.description: Multifamily loan balances rose 53% since 2019, but construction lending peaked in 2024 and declined. A capital markets analysis of bank exposure, time, and the cost of leverage.

news:news.news:title: Multifamily Lending Grew 53%. The Real Signal Is in What Shrank.
news:news.news:keywords: multifamily, bank lending, construction lending, CRE debt, capital markets

item.title: $24M Student Housing Refi: The Underwriting Condition That Made It Work
item.description: JLL secures $24M refinancing for The Row student housing near University of Florida. The three-year floating-rate loan through GID shows lenders are underwriting lease-up speed, not just location.

news:news.news:title: $24M Student Housing Refi: The Underwriting Condition That Made It Work
news:news.news:keywords: student housing, refinancing, JLL, GID, University of Florida, floating-rate loan, Ardent Cos.

item.title: Grubb's REIT Rollup Is a Time Trade. The Structure Is the Strategy.
item.description: Grubb Properties merges funds into a $1.9B nontraded REIT, securing $617M in financing. The consolidation buys time, simplifies the capital stack, and reveals how structure manages maturity risk.

news:news.news:title: Grubb's REIT Rollup Is a Time Trade. The Structure Is the Strategy.
news:news.news:keywords: Grubb Properties, nontraded REIT, multifamily, capital stack, recapitalization, time cost, Maxim Capital Group

item.title: Can a 97,285-SF Lease in West Houston Prove Industrial Demand Is Still Priced for Growth?
item.description: Cooler Master Corp. signs 97,285 SF industrial lease at Grand West Crossing in West Houston. JLL represents EastGroup Properties. Analysis of tenant demand, landlord basis, and capital implications.

news:news.news:title: Can a 97,285-SF Lease in West Houston Prove Industrial Demand Is Still Priced for Growth?
news:news.news:keywords: industrial, Houston, lease, EastGroup Properties, Cooler Master

item.title: A Credit Committee Approved This Loan. Here's What They Had to Believe.
item.description: Webster Bank's $26.3M refi of The Bridgeline in the Bronx shows lenders are underwriting time, not just basis. A two-year fixed-rate loan on a 2018-vintage asset reveals the conditions that separate investable deals from attractive stories.

news:news.news:title: A Credit Committee Approved This Loan. Here's What They Had to Believe.
news:news.news:keywords: Webster Bank, JCAL Development, The Bridgeline, Bronx multifamily, refinancing, Cushman & Wakefield, two-year loan, fixed-rate, underwriting

item.title: Time Is the Most Expensive Ingredient in This Industrial Lease
item.description: American Eagle's 472,890 SF industrial lease in Salisbury, NC, after a multi-year search across three states, shows how lease duration has become a capital allocation decision for retailers.

news:news.news:title: Time Is the Most Expensive Ingredient in This Industrial Lease
news:news.news:keywords: industrial lease, American Eagle, Cushman & Wakefield, Charlotte, distribution center, lease duration

item.title: Can a Speculative Industrial Project in Houston Find Tenants Before the Debt Clock Runs?
item.description: HLCI completes Par 1960, a 254,186 SF speculative industrial project in northwest Houston. The lease-up pace will reveal whether lender appetite for spec construction remains intact.

news:news.news:title: Can a Speculative Industrial Project in Houston Find Tenants Before the Debt Clock Runs?
news:news.news:keywords: HLCI, Houston industrial, speculative development, lease-up risk, construction debt

item.title: A 102-Unit Affordable Project Closed in 90 Days. Here's What the Lender Had to Believe.
item.description: Steve Ballmer-backed Washington Family Housing Fund closes first deal in 90 days without LIHTC. A $13.41M forgivable loan and recycled bonds show how philanthropic capital can reshape affordable housing finance.

news:news.news:title: A 102-Unit Affordable Project Closed in 90 Days. Here's What the Lender Had to Believe.
news:news.news:keywords: affordable housing, philanthropic capital, Washington Family Housing Fund, Ballmer Group, LIHTC, tax-exempt bonds, Fannie Mae, Great Expectations

item.title: What a $141M Refi Tells Lenders About the Cost of Time in Manhattan Multifamily
item.description: M&T Realty Capital provides $141.4M bridge loan on Anagram Turtle Bay. The three-year gap between JV formation and refi reveals how time reshapes leverage, basis, and lender appetite.

news:news.news:title: What a $141M Refi Tells Lenders About the Cost of Time in Manhattan Multifamily
news:news.news:keywords: M&T Realty Capital, Anagram Turtle Bay, Manhattan multifamily, bridge financing, cost of time

item.title: SROA's $750M Storage Fund Looks Like a Bet on Scale. It's a Bet on Operating Leverage.
item.description: SROA Capital launches Fund X targeting $750M for value-add self-storage. The thesis: operating leverage, not cap rate compression, drives returns in a fragmented market.

news:news.news:title: SROA's $750M Storage Fund Looks Like a Bet on Scale. It's a Bet on Operating Leverage.
news:news.news:keywords: self-storage, SROA Capital, value-add, fundraising, operating leverage, NOI growth

item.title: Brennan Bought a Fully Leased Idaho Portfolio. Here's What They Had to Believe.
item.description: Brennan Investment Group buys 439,600 SF industrial portfolio in Nampa, ID. The deal reveals the underwriting conditions separating investable assets from attractive stories in today's industrial market.

news:news.news:title: Brennan Bought a Fully Leased Idaho Portfolio. Here's What They Had to Believe.
news:news.news:keywords: Brennan Investment Group, industrial real estate, Boise market, Nampa Idaho, underwriting discipline

item.title: Sale-Leasebacks Are Pricing Time, Not Just Real Estate
item.description: Partners Real Estate brokers a sale-leaseback of a 55,700-SF industrial building in New Caney, Texas. The undisclosed price and buyer reveal how capital is pricing operational continuity over asset speculation.

news:news.news:title: Sale-Leasebacks Are Pricing Time, Not Just Real Estate
news:news.news:keywords: sale-leaseback, industrial, Houston, capital markets, liquidity

item.title: Developers Are Leasing Before They Finish. That Is the Signal.
item.description: Rosewood begins leasing 359-unit Phase III of Tobin Estates in San Antonio. The move signals how construction lenders are managing completion risk and liquidity in 2026.

news:news.news:title: Developers Are Leasing Before They Finish. That Is the Signal.
news:news.news:keywords: Rosewood Property Co., Tobin Estates, San Antonio, multifamily development, construction lending

item.title: The Deal Looked Like a Sale. It Was a Liquidity Trade.
item.description: Marcus & Millichap sold a 72%-occupied retail center in Prairieville, La. The buyer, an East Coast investor, paid for optionality, not yield. A signal for secondary-market retail pricing.

news:news.news:title: The Deal Looked Like a Sale. It Was a Liquidity Trade.
news:news.news:keywords: retail, liquidity, secondary market, basis, Marcus & Millichap

item.title: What East Harlem's B-East RFP Tells Developers About the Cost of City Land
item.description: NYC's B-East RFP demands 140 affordable homes under 485-x with no subsidy. Developers must weigh construction costs, zoning, and exit risk against a vacant lot that has waited 18 years.

news:news.news:title: What East Harlem's B-East RFP Tells Developers About the Cost of City Land
news:news.news:keywords: NYC development, affordable housing, 485-x, land basis, RFP, East Harlem
